title: 10 best herbal tinctures for nausea
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for nausea are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, used to alleviate symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and digestive discomfort. These natural remedies help by calming the stomach, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Examples include ginger, peppermint, and lemon balm tinctures, which have been used for centuries to soothe stomach issues. For instance, a woman who suffers from morning sickness during pregnancy can benefit from taking ginger tincture before meals, improving her appetite and overall well-being. This natural approach empowers individuals to take control of their digestive health and regain confidence in their daily lives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique ability to interact with the digestive system. The bioactive compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with nausea, including stomach discomfort and bloating. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help to stimulate digestion and relieve feelings of queasiness, providing quick relief from nausea caused by various triggers, including motion sickness, morning sickness during pregnancy, or food poisoning.
remedy_properties: Volatile oils: Ginger's volatile oils, such as gingerol and shogaol, have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines that may be contributing to nausea.

Shogaol: Shogaol is a bioactive compound found in ginger that has been shown to have potent anti-emetic properties, meaning it can help to alleviate vomiting and nausea by blocking serotonin receptors in the brain.

Gingerols: Gingerols are another type of active compound found in ginger that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help to reduce nausea caused by inflammation or irritation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties, making them effective in soothing nausea.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain a high concentration of shogaols and gingerols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and can help alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root from your garden or store-bought organic variety for processing.
Chop the harvested ginger into small pieces weighing approximately 50 grams and transfer to a glass jar.
Fill the jar with 95% ethanol at room temperature for a 4-hour maceration period allowing solutes to dissolve.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing the mixture to steep and extract active compounds slowly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids and label the tincture for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique ability to calm the digestive system. The menthol content in peppermint oil relaxes the stomach muscles, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to queasiness. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce gut irritation and soothe the stomach lining, further alleviating nausea symptoms. By calming the digestive tract, peppermint tinctures can provide quick and effective relief from nausea and vomiting, making it a popular natural remedy for morning sickness and other stomach woes.
remedy_properties: Digestive Soothing: Herbal peppermint tincture helps to calm digestive issues, including nausea, by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and reducing inflammation, making it easier for the body to digest food.
Antispasmodic Properties: Peppermint tincture's antispasmodic properties help to relieve muscle spasms in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.

Vasoconstrictive Effects: The menthol content in peppermint tincture helps to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce inflammation and swelling in the gut, thereby alleviating nausea-inducing conditions such as IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome).
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain menthol, which helps to calm the digestive system and alleviate nausea.
Leaves: Fresh or dried peppermint leaves have been traditionally used to soothe stomach upset and ease nausea due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source in the morning when oils are most concentrated.
Cleanly dry harvested peppermint leaves using paper towels or clean cloth and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 250ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol with 25g of dried peppermint leaves in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until liquid reaches desired strength and color.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth into dark glass bottle, discarding solids and labeling with date prepared and recommended dosages.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with nausea because its calming properties soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. The herb's apigenin content has been shown to bind to benzodiazepine receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-induced nausea. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate digestive discomfort and ease cramps, providing swift relief from nausea and vomiting. As a natural and gentle remedy, chamomile tinctures offer a safe and effective way to find fast relief from queasy feelings.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Chamomile tincture helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can ease cramps and spasms associated with nausea.
Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Anxiolytic property: The calming effects of chamomile tincture can help to alleviate anxiety and stress-related nausea, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, which have been shown to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Roots: Contain apigenin and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate stomach upset and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and clean them thoroughly removing any debris or stems.
Combine 1 part dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks allowing it to macerate and infuse.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the final tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids storing them in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the stomach lining. The tincture's bitter compounds also stimulate digestive enzymes and help to reduce inflammation in the gut, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, dandelion's high concentration of antioxidants protects the stomach from oxidative stress, further reducing inflammation and promoting overall gut health. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to alleviate nausea and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Dandelion tincture stimulates the liver to produce more bile, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting by improving digestion and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Antispasmodic properties: The bioactive compounds in dandelion tincture have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle tissue, which can help calm stomach cramps, spasms, and other digestive discomforts that contribute to nausea.

Inflammation-reducing properties: Dandelion tincture contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can exacerbate nausea, bloating, and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their bitter and astringent properties, dandelion roots help to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant contain gentle yet effective anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe an upset stomach and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves according to sustainable wildcrafting practices.
Clean the harvested plant material thoroughly by gently rinsing with filtered water.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces then soak in 80-proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part plant to 2 parts liquid for four weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the tincture from the solids and discard the solids.
Bottle the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to calm the stomach and alleviate digestive discomfort. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce symptoms of nausea. Additionally, lemon balm's sweet, citrusy flavor can be very comforting and relaxing, making it an effective remedy for those experiencing morning sickness or post-operative nausea. By reducing anxiety and calming the digestive system, herbal lemon balm tinctures can provide quick relief from nausea and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic properties: Lemon balm's anticholinergic properties can help relieve nausea by blocking the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that can stimulate nausea and vomiting.
Anxiolytic properties: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects of lemon balm can help alleviate nausea caused by anxiety or stress, which are common triggers for nausea.

Carminative properties: Lemon balm's carminative properties can help relieve nausea and vomiting by reducing spasms in the digestive tract and promoting the release of gas, which can contribute to feelings of discomfort and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm are used to create tinctures that help alleviate nausea due to their high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are a popular choice for making tinctures as they contain a significant amount of rosmarinic acid, a compound that has been shown to have anti-nausea and anti-emetic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop 4 oz of harvested leaves and combine with 1 cup of high-proof vodka.
Store mixture in a glass jar and allow it to steep for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain mixture through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard solids.
Transfer strained liquid into smaller glass bottles and label for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea symptoms. The tincture's ability to calm digestive issues may also be attributed to its gentle sedative effects on the nervous system, allowing it to regulate digestive rhythms and alleviate feelings of queasiness. As a result, maidenhair tree tinctures have been traditionally used to alleviate morning sickness, travel-induced nausea, and other forms of nausea-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and nausea.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, including ginkgolic acids and bilobalide, may reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea by blocking prostaglandins' action on gut smooth muscle.
Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's vasodilatory effects can help improve blood flow to the brain and other organs, which may help reduce nausea by increasing oxygenation and nutrient supply.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues that can contribute to nausea.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that have been traditionally used to calm the stomach and alleviate symptoms of nausea, such as discomfort and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems.
Combine the ginkgo biloba mixture with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for six to eight hours or overnight before filtering.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator and allow it to mature for at least two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique composition, which includes curcuminoids that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken as a supplement, these compounds work to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of nausea and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's natural ability to soothe and calm the stomach helps to ease queasiness and reduce the frequency and severity of nausea episodes.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties: Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract.

Ginger's natural antispasmodic properties: Ginger, another key component of herbal turmeric tinctures, contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that help relax smooth muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, alleviating nausea and vomiting.

Turmerics' ability to stimulate digestion: Turmeric's bioactive compounds, including curcumin, have been shown to enhance digestive enzyme production, increase gut motility, and improve nutrient absorption, which can help alleviate nausea by promoting a healthy digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps alleviate nausea.
Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes contain the highest amount of curcuminoids, making them an ideal choice for extracting medicinal compounds to combat nausea.
remedy_recipe: Gather one pound of dried turmeric root powder and two cups of 80-proof vodka.
Combine the turmeric root powder with two cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Stir the mixture thoroughly for three minutes to ensure even distribution of ingredients.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it daily for three minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with nausea because it contains a natural compound called anethole, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms of nausea. When taken as a supplement or added to food and drinks, fennel tincture may help calm upset stomachs and alleviate feelings of queasiness, making it a popular natural remedy for morning sickness, motion sickness, and other forms of nausea.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing muscle spasms and cramping that can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Carminative properties: Fennel's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and bloating from the digestive system, which can reduce symptoms of nausea and discomfort.
Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Fennel's anxiolytic effects can help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which is a common trigger for nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and bloating, due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain oils that help reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the stomach muscles and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems at the peak of freshness in morning sunlight.
Clean the harvested material thoroughly by rinsing it under cold running water gently.
Chop the cleaned foeniculum vulgare into small pieces measuring 1/4 inch for optimal extraction.
Combine the chopped foeniculum vulgare with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling to the top.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place allowing it to macerate for at least 6 weeks shaking daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with nausea because it contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the digestive system. Glycyrrhizin, a key component of licorice root, has been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea. Additionally, licorice tincture's expectorant properties can help calm the stomach and promote digestion, further reducing the occurrence of nausea. By addressing the underlying causes of nausea, herbal licorice tinctures provide natural relief from this uncomfortable condition.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stomach irritation and inflammation that may be contributing to nausea.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate nausea.
Saponins: Saponins in licorice root have anti-emetic properties, meaning they can help reduce the frequency and severity of vomiting episodes associated with nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach, making them effective in treating nausea.
Bark: The bark is used to create a tincture that can help to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea by its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in sufficient quantity for the desired yield of tincture.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks, shaking the jar daily, ensuring optimal extraction of medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding solids and reserving the tincture.
Store the glycyrrhiza glabra tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, allowing it to mature for at least six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anise tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique ability to soothe the digestive system. The oil extracted from the anise plant, also known as Pimpinella anisum, contains compounds that have a calming effect on the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps. This gentle relief allows the body to rebalance its natural digestive processes, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. As a result, anise tinctures can provide quick and effective relief from morning sickness, motion sickness, and other forms of nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Anise's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anethole and terpinen-4-ol, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Carminative properties: Anise's essential oils, including anethole and linalool, have a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, helping to relieve gas and bloating that can exacerbate nausea.

Antispasmodic properties: Anise's antispasmodic compounds, such as estragole and dillapiol, help relax smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can lead to nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the anise plant are rich in compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them effective in reducing nausea and vomiting.
Seeds: Anise seeds contain a natural oil that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pimpinella anisum seeds in bulk to ensure sufficient quantity for the tincture.
Clean and dry the pimpinella anisum seeds thoroughly to remove any debris or moisture.
Combine 250ml of vodka with 30g of dried pimpinella anisum seeds in a glass jar for maceration.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 14 days of steeping.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nausea with herbal tinctures is to start by using a combination of ginger and peppermint. Mix 10-15 drops of each tincture in water or tea three times a day. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to settle the stomach, while peppermint soothes the digestive tract. This potent blend can quickly alleviate nausea and reduce discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nausea that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Other conditions like vertigo, migraines, and stress-induced anxiety can also be alleviated with the use of herbal tinctures. For example, peppermint oil tincture can help soothe irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms, while ginger tincture can combat nausea and vomiting associated with morning sickness or chemotherapy.
